hard.next_step: RFI received 2 July (Tail Spend / ANA / AI), NDA to be signed, response due 24 July 2026.
hard.ais_why: Bogdan Marius N. (Pirelli Sourcing Excellence) stated market-scouting for AI procurement tools, called out autonomous sourcing as a priority, and identified AI automation of sub-threshold purchase orders; ANA is embedded in the opp name and Agentic AI + Merlin are in scope.

north_star_verdict.headline: Healthy and re-warming: after a 2.5-month cooling and near qualify-out in June, Pirelli's Digital Purchasing team issued a formal RFI on 2 July centred on tail-spend, ANA and AI, response due 24 July. The deal is genuinely alive and the RFI mirrors our differentiator, but at Qualified with only the 19 March intro actually held (the 9 April demo was cancelled), no economic buyer mapped and no pricing, a full enterprise motion cannot complete by the 31 Dec placeholder date.
north_star_verdict.math: 178 days to close (31 Dec 2026). Created 27 March 2026, 101 days old; Qualified since 31 March, no stage progression. All submission dates (Qualified_Submission_Date__c, Formal_Eval_Submission_Date__c, Shortlisted_Submission_Date__c) null. Last buyer touch 2 July 2026 (4 days ago, LIVE). Real calls held: one (intro 19 March); the 9 April demo was cancelled by the buyer and never rescheduled. To hold a 2026 close the RFI must convert to a live demo and formal evaluation within 30 days and multi-thread past a single functional contact; the 31 Dec date is optimistic for an enterprise S2C+ANA cycle just entering RFI.
evidence: Pulse LIVE: LastActivityDate 2 July 2026. Pirelli issued a formal Request for Information on 2 July (Giacomo Ginanni, Digital Purchasing Team): 'Request for Information (RFI) - Autonomous Sourcing & Procurement Automation - Prequalification process' (Task 2 July 2026).
RFI is oriented on our exact wedge: 'RFI oriented on Tail Spend Management / ANA / AI is critical', NDA to be signed, response due 24 July (Next_Step__c, 2 July 2026).

Rocky run-up: buyer went quiet after the intro, '3 reminders sent... if no news by end of June => Qualified out' (Next_Step__c, 15 June 2026), then re-engaged: 'We are currently still bringing forward this project... Sorry for not getting back to you in the past weeks, but they were quite busy' (Email, 17 June 2026).

The 9 April 90-minute demo was cancelled by the buyer: 'we have to kindly ask you to please cancel today's session as we have to deal with an urgent topic internally' (Email, 9 April 2026); it was never rescheduled, so the only substantive conversation remains the 19 March intro.

Decision Maker Bogdan Marius N. (Global Head of Sourcing Excellence, OpportunityContactRole primary) has never appeared on a call or email; all live contact runs through Andrea Biagini and now Giacomo Ginanni.
No Economic Buyer mapped and no pricing, budget or commercial discussion on record; value is unquantified beyond the rep's illustrative tail-spend math on 19 March.
Displacement play: Pirelli runs sourcing and supplier management on SynerTrade and archives contracts on OpenText, with contract negotiation entirely offline (Avoma intro, 19 March 2026).

metrics.narrative: Andrea Biagini articulated a clear qualitative pain on the 19 March intro: a dedicated team is consumed managing sub-threshold purchase orders that Pirelli wants to automate 'to revert that team to more edit-value activities', and contract negotiation is 'currently offline, we don't have a dedicated tool'. Pierre Meraud offered the value frame that tail-spend is roughly 20% of spend and a 2-3% negotiated saving on it 'can be impressive numbers for a company the size of Pirelli' (Avoma intro, 19 March 2026), but Pirelli has not quantified anything: no baseline PO volume, no cost-per-transaction, no FTE count, no target saving. The 2 July RFI signals appetite but still carries no numbers. Quantifying the tail-spend savings and FTE reallocation inside the RFI response is the move.
sources: Avoma intro call, Andrea Biagini + Pierre Meraud, 19 March 2026
Next_Step__c, 27 March 2026 (automate sub-threshold POs to reallocate resources)
Next_Step__c, 2 July 2026 (RFI oriented on Tail Spend Management)

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is identified and no one has discussed budget, pricing or commercial authority on any call or email. Bogdan Marius N. (Global Head of Sourcing Excellence) is the named primary Decision Maker on the OpportunityContactRole and the most senior person mapped, and Andrea's cost-effectiveness/purchasing-intelligence remit rolls up into his sourcing-excellence team, but Bogdan has never engaged directly. For a 200K S2C+ANA deal the budget owner is likely a CPO or CFO who is entirely unmapped. The evidence shows only functional sponsorship (Andrea) and now a procurement-process contact (Giacomo Ginanni); it shows nothing about who signs. Using the RFI response to request a scoping call with Bogdan and the budget owner is the move to close this.
sources: OpportunityContactRole: Bogdan Marius N., Decision Maker, primary
OpportunityContactRole: Andrea Biagini, Evaluator
Task/email trail 15 June - 2 July 2026 (no budget or pricing discussion)

decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer's evaluation criteria are consistent across two dated sources and centre on our strengths. On 19 March Andrea named the target areas: contract lifecycle management (offline today), sourcing to replace SynerTrade, procurement/PO automation, and explicitly 'autonomous sourcing as well'. The 2 July RFI then formalised those into a stated scope: 'RFI oriented on Tail Spend Management / ANA / AI is critical' (Next_Step__c, 2 July 2026). Confirmed criteria: tail-spend automation, autonomous negotiation (ANA) and AI. Still inferred rather than confirmed: how Pirelli will weight contract-lifecycle vs sourcing displacement, and any scoring rubric inside the prequalification RFI, which we should ask Giacomo to share.
sources: Avoma intro call, Andrea Biagini, 19 March 2026
Next_Step__c, 2 July 2026 (RFI scope: Tail Spend / ANA / AI)
Task 2 July 2026 (RFI - Autonomous Sourcing & Procurement Automation - Prequalification process)

decision_process.narrative: Reading the sequence itself: an exploratory intro on 19 March (Andrea Biagini with Pierre Meraud, Hemendra Chaturvedi and presales Luis) led to a 90-minute demo booked for 9 April, which the buyer cancelled on the day for an 'urgent topic internally' and never rebooked. A 2.5-month quiet spell followed with three chase reminders and a near qualify-out, then the buyer re-engaged in mid-to-late June ('still bringing forward this project'), and on 2 July Pirelli's Digital Purchasing team opened a formal RFI and prequalification with an NDA and a 24 July response deadline. So the process has just shifted from informal exploration to a procurement-run RFI. The approval chain (Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c, Multiple_approvals__c) is not yet visible and should be asked of Giacomo.
sources: Avoma intro call, 19 March 2026
Email, 9 April 2026 (demo cancelled by buyer)
Next_Step__c, 15 June 2026 (3 reminders / near qualify-out)
Task/Next_Step__c, 2 July 2026 (formal RFI + prequalification, response due 24 July)

paper_process.narrative: The first contracting signal has just appeared: the RFI carries an NDA 'to be Signed' as part of the prequalification (Next_Step__c, 2 July 2026), which gates deeper information exchange. Beyond the NDA there is no MSA, order form, SOW, legal or InfoSec activity yet, which is normal this early. The SynerTrade contract-expiration date is not captured and would be a forcing function worth uncovering (Current_Contract_Expiration__c is not populated). Getting the mutual NDA executed and asking Giacomo for the RFI timeline and required approvals is what creates paper-process visibility.
sources: Next_Step__c, 2 July 2026 (NDA to be signed)
Task 2 July 2026 (prequalification process)

identify_pain.narrative: Pirelli's pain is confirmed and specific, articulated by Andrea Biagini on 19 March. First, sub-threshold tail-spend POs are handled by a dedicated team Pirelli wants freed for higher-value work. Second, contract negotiation is entirely manual: 'everything to the contractual negotiation, both internal and external, is currently offline, we don't have a dedicated tool... we are also looking into that as soon as possible'. Third, the incumbent hurts them: on SynerTrade 'we personalized data... so we cannot take advantage of the regular updates', a customization lock-in the Zycus presales consultant confirmed is a recurring SynerTrade issue. The pain owner is Andrea Biagini (Cost Effectiveness & Purchasing Intelligence). It is operational and real but not yet escalated to a CFO-grade financial case.
sources: Avoma intro call, Andrea Biagini, 19 March 2026
Next_Step__c, 27 March 2026

champion.narrative: Andrea Biagini (Cost Effectiveness & Purchasing Intelligence, Evaluator) is a developing champion, not yet a proven one. What has developed: Andrea initiated the outreach, ran the 19 March intro, laid out Pirelli's pain candidly, and kept the project alive through internal delays ('We are currently still bringing forward this project', 17 June 2026). What is still missing: no introduction to the Decision Maker Bogdan Marius N., no economic buyer, no internal business case shared, and the 9 April demo cancelled on Andrea's side was never rescheduled. A second procurement contact, Giacomo Ginanni (Digital Purchasing Team), has now entered via the 2 July RFI, which broadens the thread but is a formal procurement channel, not yet advocacy. Securing Andrea as an active internal seller and converting Giacomo into a working contact is the champion move.
sources: OpportunityContactRole: Andrea Biagini, Evaluator
Email, 17 June 2026 (re-engagement)
Task 2 July 2026 (Giacomo Ginanni RFI)

competition.narrative: This is a displacement play against an incumbent stack. SynerTrade runs Pirelli's sourcing and supplier management today and is the named SF competitor; the wedge is Andrea's own complaint that heavy customization blocks platform updates ('we personalized data... we cannot take advantage of the regular updates', 19 March 2026), which the Zycus consultant flagged as a chronic SynerTrade problem. OpenText is the incumbent contract repository (a document-management point tool, not a full S2P rival) that iContract displaces, and contract negotiation is otherwise offline. The Do-Nothing / inertia threat that dominated in June has receded now that Pirelli has issued a formal RFI, though it remains real given the project's stop-start history. We win by landing the RFI response on ANA autonomous tail-spend negotiation and Merlin on a single unified S2P database, the capabilities the customization-locked SynerTrade stack cannot match.
sources: SF Competitors__c: SynerTrade
Avoma intro call, Andrea Biagini, 19 March 2026 (SynerTrade + OpenText + offline contracts)
Next_Step__c, 2 July 2026 (RFI reframes to active evaluation, inertia receding)

critical_signals.text: Pirelli issued a formal RFI on 2 July centred on tail-spend management, autonomous negotiation and AI, with an NDA to sign and a response due 24 July.
Giacomo Ginanni from Pirelli's Digital Purchasing team surfaced on 2 July driving the RFI and prequalification, a fresh procurement contact we have engaged by email but never met.
SynerTrade is the incumbent for sourcing and supplier management, and Andrea's complaint that customization blocks platform updates is our clearest displacement wedge.
The only real conversation remains the 19 March intro; the 9 April demo was cancelled by the buyer for an urgent internal topic and was never rescheduled.
No budget owner is identified and no pricing has been discussed, and the tail-spend value case is still unquantified by Pirelli.

deal_movement.summary: The deal re-warmed sharply in early July after nearly being qualified out in mid-June. A formal RFI oriented on tail-spend, ANA and AI arrived on 2 July from Pirelli's Digital Purchasing team, with an NDA and a 24 July response deadline, following a mid-June re-engagement and a long quiet spell after the cancelled 9 April demo.

90-minute demo cancelled by the buyer for an urgent internal topic; never rescheduled

competitive_position.summary: SynerTrade is the single strongest current threat: it is the incumbent for Pirelli's sourcing and supplier management (SF Competitors__c; confirmed on the 19 March intro), and the deal is a displacement against it. Our wedge is Andrea's own admission that SynerTrade's customization blocks platform updates. OpenText is the incumbent contract repository (a point tool iContract displaces, low threat). Do-Nothing / inertia was the dominant risk through June but has receded now that Pirelli issued a formal RFI on 2 July, though its stop-start history keeps it a live watch.

competitors.name: SynerTrade
competitors.quote: The main problems we are facing with the platform we use today is that we personalized data, so we cannot take advantage of the regular updates.
competitors.source: Avoma intro call, Andrea Biagini, 19 March 2026; SF Competitors__c
competitors.how_we_win: Attack the customization lock-in: position ANA autonomous negotiation and Merlin on a single unified S2P database that upgrades without re-customization.

competitors.name: OpenText
competitors.quote: For contract lifecycle it's pretty much an offline process; for the archive of contracts we are regarding on OpenText.
competitors.source: Avoma intro call, Andrea Biagini, 19 March 2026
competitors.how_we_win: Replace a passive document repository with iContract active contract lifecycle management tied into sourcing and intake.

competitors.name: Do Nothing / Inertia
competitors.quote: We are currently still bringing forward this project. Sorry for not getting back to you in the past weeks, but they were quite busy.
competitors.how_we_win: The 2 July RFI creates a forcing date (24 July); tie a quantified tail-spend saving to a concrete 2026 milestone so status-quo has a visible cost.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Pirelli's stated expectations map tightly onto Zycus's differentiated scope: tail-spend automation, autonomous negotiation (ANA) and AI, on a single unified suite replacing a customization-locked incumbent. Fit on AI and ANA is strong; fit on the SAP S/4HANA landscape and full commercial scope is still to be validated.

best_practice_check.summary: The RFI is the highest-leverage moment on this deal; win it by converting a formal document exercise into live scoping and multi-threaded access before the 24 July deadline.
flags: Land the RFI response on the ANA tail-spend automation wedge SynerTrade cannot match, and ask Giacomo Ginanni for the scoring rubric and evaluation timeline.
Multi-thread beyond Andrea: use the RFI to get a working three-way with Decision Maker Bogdan Marius N. and to map the CPO/CFO budget owner.
Reschedule the cancelled 9 April demo as an ANA/Merlin tail-spend showcase so evaluation rests on a live experience, not just paper.

The new procurement contact Giacomo Ginanni broadens the thread but is a formal channel, not advocacy.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning is landing: the buyer's own RFI is oriented on ANA and AI, and Pierre's 19 March narrative on Merlin intake, autonomous negotiation and 50+ production agents mapped directly onto Andrea's stated tail-spend automation goal. The next step is to prove it live rather than on paper.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 8 / Strong is corroborated by the calls and the 2 July RFI: Andrea named 'market scouting for AI procurement tools' and 'autonomous sourcing', and the RFI explicitly flags ANA and AI as critical. The field is accurately rated, not over-stated.

31 Dec close is optimistic for an enterprise S2C+ANA cycle just entering RFI with no demo yet completed.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A re-warming AI-hungry displacement play: Pirelli just issued an RFI on our exact wedge (ANA tail-spend automation), but it sits at Qualified with only one real call held, no economic buyer, and a Decision Maker who has never engaged.

win_position.text: We are well positioned on fit: Pirelli's own 2 July RFI is oriented on tail-spend, ANA and AI, the exact differentiators Pierre pitched on 19 March, and the incumbent SynerTrade is hobbled by customization lock-in Andrea herself flagged.
But no economic buyer is mapped and Decision Maker Bogdan Marius N. has never appeared on a call or email, so the deal is single-threaded through a functional sponsor with no confirmed budget.
Only the 19 March intro was ever held; the 9 April demo was cancelled by the buyer and never rescheduled, so the evaluation still rests on paper and a stop-start history keeps inertia a real risk.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum turned up on 2 July when Pirelli issued a formal RFI with a customer-named 24 July response deadline, reversing a June cooling that nearly qualified the deal out.
A new procurement contact, Giacomo Ginanni, entered via the RFI, broadening engagement beyond Andrea for the first time.
No senior buyer has joined and no pricing, legal or commercial track has opened, so the process is only just formalising.

customer_commitment.text: Pirelli committed to a formal RFI and prequalification with an NDA to sign, a real procurement step rather than passive interest (2 July 2026).
Commitment is still procurement-level: the Decision Maker is absent, no budget is confirmed, and the buyer's June message ('sorry, they were quite busy') showed competing internal priorities.

deal_risk.text: The 31 Dec close is not credible for an enterprise S2C+ANA cycle just entering RFI with no demo completed and no commercials started.
Single-thread and unmapped power are the core early risks: if Andrea stalls or the RFI lacks executive sponsorship, the deal reverts to inertia.

win_position.evidence: Strong product fit and an on-wedge RFI, offset by no EB, unengaged DM, single-thread and only one call held.

deal_momentum.evidence: Customer-issued RFI with a 24 July deadline and a new stakeholder reversed a June cooling.

customer_commitment.evidence: Formal RFI + NDA is real but procurement-level; no budget or senior sponsor.

deal_risk.evidence: Placeholder EOY close, unmapped power, stop-start history.

customer_preference.evidence: Pirelli issued an RFI centred on ANA and AI (our differentiator) with a firm deadline, a positive lean, but as a prequalification RFI it is not yet a decisive Zycus preference (2 July 2026).

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain confirmed (manual tail-spend POs, offline contracting, SynerTrade customization lock-in) but no numbers from the buyer; only the rep's illustrative 20% tail-spend / 2-3% saving frame (19 March 2026).
